properties.name: Shanghai
properties.summary: World's busiest container port, handling roughly 10% of global container traffic. A manufacturing and export megahub for China's Yangtze River Delta industrial region.
properties.topExports: Electronics, Machinery, Textiles, Chemicals, Plastics
properties.topImports: Iron ore, Crude oil, Soybeans, Copper, Semiconductors

properties.name: Singapore
properties.summary: The world's second-busiest container port and premier transshipment hub connecting East Asia, South Asia, and Europe. Singapore's port handles cargo from over 600 ports in 120 countries.
properties.topExports: Refined petroleum, Electronics, Chemicals, Machinery, Gold
properties.topImports: Crude oil, Electronics, Machinery, Refined petroleum, Aircraft parts

properties.name: Rotterdam
properties.summary: Europe's largest port and its primary gateway for bulk commodities, petroleum, and containers. Rotterdam is the continent's biggest refining and petrochemical complex.
properties.topExports: Petroleum products, Chemicals, Machinery, Agri-products, Metal scrap
properties.topImports: Crude oil, Coal, Iron ore, Grain, Containers

properties.name: Busan
properties.summary: South Korea's largest port and a key transshipment hub for Northeast Asia. Busan connects Korea's export-oriented automotive and electronics industries to global markets.
properties.topExports: Automobiles, Electronics, Machinery, Steel, Ships
properties.topImports: Crude oil, LNG, Coal, Iron ore, Semiconductors

properties.name: Hong Kong
properties.summary: A major free-trade transshipment hub for southern China and the Pearl River Delta. Hong Kong's port benefits from its deep natural harbour and proximity to the world's largest manufacturing region.
properties.topExports: Electronics, Machinery, Garments, Jewellery, Plastics
properties.topImports: Electronics, Machinery, Petroleum products, Foodstuffs, Metals

properties.name: Los Angeles
properties.summary: The busiest container port in the US and the primary gateway for US consumer goods imports from Asia. Combined with neighboring Long Beach, it forms the largest port complex in the Western Hemisphere.
properties.topExports: Aircraft parts, Waste paper, Agricultural products, Cotton, Chemicals
properties.topImports: Consumer electronics, Clothing & apparel, Furniture, Automobiles, Machinery

properties.name: Jebel Ali (Dubai)
properties.summary: The Middle East's largest port and the world's largest man-made harbour. Jebel Ali serves as the transshipment hub for the Gulf region and a key BRI (Belt and Road) logistics node.
properties.topExports: Gold, Petroleum products, Aluminium, Diamonds, Machinery re-exports
properties.topImports: Machinery, Electronics, Gold, Vehicles, Pearls & precious stones

properties.name: Antwerp
properties.summary: Europe's second-largest port by volume and the world's largest petrochemical complex outside Houston. Antwerp's central position gives deep-sea vessels direct access to the Rhine-Meuse-Scheldt delta hinterland.
properties.topExports: Chemicals, Petroleum products, Vehicles, Machinery, Pharmaceuticals
properties.topImports: Crude oil, LNG, Chemicals, Vehicles, Electronics

properties.name: New York / Newark
properties.summary: The largest US East Coast port complex, serving the most populous US market. Post-Panama Canal expansion it can now receive ultra-large container vessels, boosting its import capacity.
properties.topExports: Scrap metal, Waste paper, Chemicals, Machinery, Agricultural products
properties.topImports: Pharmaceuticals, Vehicles, Electronics, Petroleum products, Clothing
properties.globalRank: #24 container port globally, #2 on US East Coast

properties.name: Tokyo
properties.summary: Japan's main container port and the economic heart of the Tokyo Bay industrial zone. Yokohama (co-located in the bay) handles additional bulk cargo, making the bay one of Asia's busiest port complexes.
properties.topExports: Automobiles, Electronics, Machinery, Precision instruments, Steel
properties.topImports: LNG, Crude oil, Coal, Foodstuffs, Chemicals
properties.globalRank: #14 container port in Japan's Tokyo Bay complex

properties.name: Mumbai (JNPT)
properties.summary: India's busiest container port via Jawaharlal Nehru Port Trust (JNPT). Mumbai is India's financial capital and the gateway for the country's vast western hinterland and manufacturing belt.
properties.topExports: Textiles & garments, Pharmaceuticals, Chemicals, Engineering goods, Gems
properties.topImports: Crude oil, Electronics, Gold, Coal, Machinery

properties.name: Santos
properties.summary: Latin America's largest port and Brazil's primary export gateway. Santos handles a massive bulk trade in agricultural commodities from Brazil's interior, especially soy, sugar, and orange juice.
properties.topExports: Soybeans, Sugar, Beef & poultry, Iron ore, Coffee
properties.topImports: Fertilizers, Electronics, Machinery, Salt, Chemicals
properties.globalRank: #1 port in Latin America by cargo volume

properties.name: Ras Tanura
properties.summary: Saudi Aramco's flagship oil export terminal and the world's largest crude oil loading facility. Ras Tanura underpins global crude oil pricing and supply security.
properties.topExports: Crude oil, Refined petroleum products, LPG, Naphtha
properties.topImports: Industrial materials, Plant equipment, Food, Construction goods
properties.globalRank: World's largest crude oil export terminal

properties.name: Houston
properties.summary: The energy capital of the United States and the country's largest petrochemical export hub. Houston became the world's leading LNG exporter in 2023, reshaping global gas markets.
properties.topExports: LNG, Crude oil, Petroleum products, Petrochemicals, Cotton
properties.topImports: Crude oil, Steel, Plastics, Machinery, Coffee
properties.globalRank: #1 US port by foreign waterborne cargo tonnage

properties.name: Dalian
properties.summary: Northeast China's largest port and a key crude oil and iron ore import terminal for Chinese refineries and steel mills. Dalian also serves as the deepwater gateway for landlocked Northeast Asian markets.
properties.topExports: Machinery, Chemicals, Steel products, Petroleum products, Electronics
properties.topImports: Iron ore, Crude oil, Grain, LNG, Coal
properties.globalRank: Top 5 Chinese port by bulk cargo volume

properties.name: Port Hedland
properties.summary: The world's largest bulk export port by tonnage, shipping Pilbara iron ore almost exclusively to East Asian steel mills. Dust storms from ore stockpiles are visible from space.
properties.topExports: Iron ore, Salt, Manganese ore
properties.topImports: Diesel fuel, Industrial equipment, Explosives

properties.name: Richards Bay
properties.summary: Sub-Saharan Africa's largest port by volume and the continent's premier coal export terminal. Richards Bay is also a key exporter of mineral sands, aluminium, and ferrochrome.
properties.topExports: Thermal coal, Mineral sands, Aluminium, Ferrochrome, Woodchips
properties.topImports: Equipment, Fuel, Chemicals, Machinery

properties.name: Tubarão (Vitória)
properties.summary: Operated by Vale, Tubarão is one of the world's largest iron ore export terminals. The port ships ore from Brazil's Carajás mines on a 40-day voyage around the Cape to Chinese steel mills.
properties.topExports: Iron ore pellets, Steel slabs, Iron ore
properties.topImports: Coal, Coke, Machinery, Limestone
properties.globalRank: World's 2nd largest iron ore export terminal

properties.name: Piraeus
properties.summary: Southeastern Europe's largest container port, controlled by COSCO Shipping since 2016. Piraeus is the Belt and Road Initiative's western anchor, distributing Asian goods to the Balkans and Central Europe.
properties.topExports: Petroleum products, Chemicals, Olive oil, Pharmaceutical products, Aluminum
properties.topImports: Electronics, Machinery, Vehicles, Petroleum products, Foodstuffs

properties.name: Laem Chabang
properties.summary: Thailand's primary deep-sea container port and the industrial heartland of the Eastern Seaboard. Laem Chabang is a key export hub for Thai automobiles, electronics, and processed food.
properties.topExports: Automobiles & parts, Electronics, Processed food, Rubber, Machinery
properties.topImports: Machinery, Electronics, Petroleum products, Chemicals, Steel

properties.name: Savannah
properties.summary: The third-largest US container port and the fastest-growing major port in North America. Savannah serves the booming US Southeast manufacturing belt, including major automotive and distribution centre clusters.
properties.topExports: Paper products, Vehicles & parts, Agricultural products, Kaolin clay, Machinery
properties.topImports: Consumer goods, Furniture, Clothing & apparel, Electronics, Carpets

properties.name: Alexandria
properties.summary: Egypt's largest port and the main gateway for the country's vital food and commodity imports. Alexandria's proximity to the Suez Canal makes it a strategic node for Mediterranean and Red Sea trade.
properties.topExports: Petroleum products, Cotton, Chemicals, Citrus fruits, Marble
properties.topImports: Grain & wheat, Vehicles, Machinery, Iron & steel, Petroleum

properties.name: Novorossiysk
properties.summary: Russia's largest port by cargo volume and the primary export terminal for Urals crude oil from the Caspian pipeline. Subject to Western sanctions since 2022, Novorossiysk has pivoted to India and China.
properties.topExports: Crude oil, Grain & wheat, Coal, Metal products, Fertilizers
properties.topImports: Metals, Equipment, Foodstuffs, Consumer goods, Chemicals

properties.name: Suez Canal
properties.summary: The world's most important artificial waterway, connecting the Mediterranean to the Red Sea. Its closure forces vessels on a 10,000 km detour around Africa, adding ~2 weeks and major fuel costs.
properties.primaryCommodities: Containers, Crude oil, LNG, Grain, Petroleum products

properties.name: Panama Canal
properties.summary: The only sea route linking the Pacific and Atlantic oceans without circumnavigating Cape Horn. Its lock size limits vessel draft, making it a critical bottleneck for LNG and grain shipments from the US Gulf.

properties.name: Strait of Malacca
properties.summary: The world's busiest and most strategically vital sea lane, connecting the Indian Ocean to the South China Sea. Its closure would force oil tankers and container ships on lengthy detours through the Lombok or Sunda Straits.
properties.primaryCommodities: Crude oil, LNG, Containers, Coal, Iron ore

properties.name: Strait of Hormuz
properties.summary: The only sea exit from the Persian Gulf, through which flows roughly 21% of global oil consumption daily. Iran has repeatedly threatened closure as a geopolitical weapon, which would trigger immediate global energy shock.
properties.primaryCommodities: Crude oil, LNG, Petroleum products, LPG

properties.name: Bab el-Mandeb
properties.summary: The gateway between the Red Sea and the Gulf of Aden. Houthi missile and drone attacks since late 2023 have diverted hundreds of vessels around the Cape of Good Hope, raising insurance rates and voyage costs.
properties.primaryCommodities: Containers, Crude oil, LNG, Grain, Petroleum products

properties.name: Strait of Gibraltar
properties.summary: The narrow gateway between the Atlantic Ocean and the Mediterranean Sea. One of the world's busiest shipping lanes, it is also a key chokepoint for Russian oil shipments transiting to Atlantic markets.
properties.primaryCommodities: Containers, Crude oil, LNG, Bulk, Petroleum products

properties.name: Bosporus
properties.summary: The sole maritime exit from the Black Sea, running through Istanbul. The Turkish Straits Convention limits warship transit, while Russian oil and Ukrainian grain trade depend critically on unimpeded passage.
properties.primaryCommodities: Crude oil, Grain & wheat, Steel, Petroleum products, Coal

properties.name: Cape of Good Hope
properties.summary: The stormy southern tip of Africa and the primary bypass route when the Suez Canal or Bab el-Mandeb is disrupted. The 2023–24 Houthi crisis caused a sharp surge in Cape roundings, adding ~10 transit days per voyage.
properties.tradeSharePct: ~4–8% of global trade (rises sharply during Suez disruptions)
properties.primaryCommodities: Containers, Crude oil, Bulk, LNG, Petroleum products

properties.name: Lombok Strait
properties.summary: A deep-water alternative to the Strait of Malacca for large tankers and bulk carriers. The Lombok Strait can accommodate very large crude carriers (VLCCs) that are too large for Malacca's shallow 25m depth.
properties.primaryCommodities: Crude oil (VLCC), Iron ore, LNG, Coal, Containers

properties.name: Strait of Dover
properties.summary: The world's busiest international shipping lane, with over 500 ships per day using the separation scheme. Dover is also the UK's primary ferry link to continental Europe, carrying passengers and lorries.

properties.name: Taiwan Strait
properties.summary: One of the world's most geopolitically sensitive sea lanes, through which roughly half the global containership fleet transits annually. A Chinese blockade of Taiwan would be catastrophic for global semiconductor supply chains.

properties.name: Mozambique Channel
properties.summary: A 1,700 km-wide channel between Madagascar and mainland Africa. It serves as an alternative Cape bypass route and a key transit corridor for tankers trading between the Persian Gulf and Atlantic markets.
properties.primaryCommodities: Crude oil, LNG (Mozambique LNG), Bulk, Containers
